target: Phase-1 test-suite coverage vs plan public-API + ERROR-CATALOG + 14 invariants + 6 named compromises + 11 R1 attack vectors + 6 exit criteria + 14 R2-projected proptests
summary: Coverage is strong on the R1-named attack classes (all 11 covered by name), all 6 named compromises have regression tests, all 21 live Phase-1 error codes are fired by at least one test, all 6 exit criteria have Rust + TS assertions, and every enforced invariant (1/2/3/5/6/9/10/12 plus stopgaps 8-nest-depth and 11-system-zone) has at least a rejection + positive + diagnostic-accessor test. Three gaps block a pass verdict: (1) 8 of the 14 R2-projected / agent-definition-required Phase-1 proptests are absent — only `prop_node_roundtrip_cid_stable`, `prop_node_anchor_id_excluded_from_cid`, `prop_value_float_bits_stable`, `prop_version_chain_linearizable`, `prop_content_listing_incremental_equivalence`, `prop_noauth_returns_ok_unconditionally`, `prop_noauth_hot_path_does_not_allocate` are landed; missing include `prop_edge_roundtrip_cid_stable`, `prop_value_json_cbor_conversion`, `prop_kvbackend_put_get_delete`, `prop_transform_expression_deterministic`, `prop_subgraph_cid_order_independent` (R1 philosophy-critic finding), `prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic`, `prop_capability_check_deterministic`, `prop_hlc_monotonic`. (2) Several Phase-1 public-API methods have zero tests on disk: `Engine::update_node`, `Engine::delete_node`, `Engine::create_edge`, `Engine::get_edge`, `Engine::edges_from`, `Engine::edges_to`, `Engine::delete_edge`, `Engine::call_async`, `Engine::read_view_with`, `Engine::handler_to_mermaid`, `Engine::handler_predecessors`, `RedbBackend::get_by_label`, `RedbBackend::get_by_property`. TS binding surface B3 (`updateNode`/`deleteNode`/`edgesFrom`/`edgesTo`/`deleteEdge`) is completely untested at the napi or Vitest layer. (3) IVM hand-written views 1/2/5 only have 4 tests each — far below the plan §4.1 requirement of per-view (a) 'build from scratch matches incremental' equivalence, (b) 'stale-on-budget-exceeded', (c) 'write-read latency bound'. Only View3 approximates this (plus one proptest). View4 has four tests (cycle/truncate/cap/empty) but still lacks an incremental-equivalence proptest.

stats.public_apis_covered: ~92 / ~113 (engine surface + napi B3-B5 methods; estimate, see finding PA1 for line-item list)
stats.error_codes_fired: 21 / 21 Phase-1 codes (100%) — 8 Phase-2 codes correctly un-tested (`E_INV_SANDBOX_NESTED`, `E_INV_ITERATE_MAX_MISSING`, `E_INV_ITERATE_BUDGET`, `E_INV_SYSTEM_ZONE`, `E_SANDBOX_FUEL_EXHAUSTED`, `E_SANDBOX_TIMEOUT`, `E_SANDBOX_OUTPUT_LIMIT`, `E_CAP_REVOKED`), 3 Phase-3 sync codes un-tested as expected
stats.invariants_with_full_quartet: 8 / 8 (invariants 1/2/3/5/6/9/10/12 each have rejection + positive-at-limit + error-diagnostic-accessor tests; invariants 1/2/3/5/6 also have exact-limit and one-over tests. Invariant 8 stopgap `E_INV_ITERATE_NEST_DEPTH` and invariant 11 stopgap `E_SYSTEM_ZONE_WRITE` both have the same quartet shape.)
stats.compromises_regressed: 6 / 6 (all in `tests/integration/compromises_regression.rs`; each has `// Phase 1 compromise; remove when Phase X ...` grep-marker comment)
stats.attack_vectors_tested: 11 / 11 (R3 security writer's coverage map lists each R1 class and its named test file)
stats.proptests_landed_vs_projected: 7 / 14 projected by R2 landscape (8 of the agent-definition's Phase-1 load-bearing proptests list are missing)
stats.ci_workflows_landed_vs_projected: 8 / 9 (ci, determinism, drift-detect, wasm-checks, cross-process-graph, cold-install, supply-chain, supply-chain-seeded-test — no separate MSRV workflow file, MSRV may be a job inside ci.yml)

findings.location: workspace — 8 proptests missing from R2 §3 table (and from the rust-test-coverage agent-definition's required list)
findings.claim: Only 7 proptest functions are on disk (`prop_node_roundtrip_cid_stable`, `prop_node_anchor_id_excluded_from_cid`, `prop_value_float_bits_stable`, `prop_version_chain_linearizable`, `prop_content_listing_incremental_equivalence`, `prop_noauth_returns_ok_unconditionally`, `prop_noauth_hot_path_does_not_allocate`). The landscape specifies 14 and the rust-test-coverage agent-definition names 8 load-bearing Phase-1 proptests. Missing: `prop_edge_roundtrip_cid_stable`, `prop_value_json_cbor_conversion`, `prop_kvbackend_put_get_delete`, `prop_transform_expression_deterministic`, `prop_subgraph_cid_order_independent` (R1 benten-engine-philosophy major finding — canonicalization regression guard), `prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic`, `prop_capability_check_deterministic`, `prop_hlc_monotonic`.
findings.evidence: `grep -r 'fn prop_' /Users/benwork/Documents/benten-engine` returns 7 distinct property-test function names across `crates/benten-caps/tests/noauth_proptest.rs`, `crates/benten-core/tests/proptests.rs`, `crates/benten-ivm/tests/view3_content_listing.rs`. No hit for any of the 8 missing names. Particular concern: `prop_subgraph_cid_order_independent` was explicitly called out in R1 triage as a philosophy-critic finding to be landed; its absence leaves the subgraph canonicalization rule (Nodes sorted by CID, Edges by `(source_cid, target_cid, label)`) untested against insertion-order variance.
findings.fix: Land the 8 missing proptest harnesses in R4b or escalate to Ben. Easy wins: `prop_edge_roundtrip_cid_stable` (mirrors the node one in `crates/benten-core/tests/proptests.rs`), `prop_kvbackend_put_get_delete` (landscape §3 row 6 — MemBackend + RedbBackend). `prop_subgraph_cid_order_independent` requires a `canonical_bytes()` hook through `Subgraph::cid()` which exists on disk. `prop_transform_expression_deterministic` must wait on the TRANSFORM parser landing in R5 G6-B but should at least compile now.

findings.location: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs + bindings/napi/src/lib.rs — Engine CRUD + napi B3 surface
findings.claim: Plan N2 and B3 deliverables (full CRUD surface) have zero tests: `Engine::update_node`, `Engine::delete_node`, `Engine::create_edge`, `Engine::get_edge`, `Engine::edges_from`, `Engine::edges_to`, `Engine::delete_edge`. Napi side `updateNode`, `deleteNode`, `createEdge`, `getEdge`, `edgesFrom`, `edgesTo`, `deleteEdge` are neither declared in `bindings/napi/src/lib.rs` nor asserted in `bindings/napi/index.test.ts` / any Vitest file.
findings.evidence: `grep -rn '\bupdate_node\b\|\bdelete_node\b\|\bupdate_edge\b\|\bdelete_edge\b\|\bedgesFrom\b\|\bedgesTo\b\|\bdeleteNode\b\|\bupdateNode\b' /Users/benwork/Documents/benten-engine` returns zero matches inside `crates/` source, tests, `bindings/napi/`, and `packages/engine/`. The napi source (`bindings/napi/src/lib.rs`) only exposes `initEngine`, `createNode`, `getNode`. The Vitest `ts_full_crud_cycle_nodes` test writes + reads but does not exercise update or delete.
findings.fix: Add Rust unit tests for each of the 7 Engine methods (TDD-style, failing until R5 N2/N3 land). Add napi-rs `#[napi]` declarations and matching Vitest assertions for B3 deliverables. These are exit-criterion-adjacent: `crud('post')` zero-config implicitly needs update + delete.

findings.location: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:257–405 — engine methods added for integration harnesses
findings.claim: Several public methods on `Engine` added during R3 integration scaffolding have zero direct unit test on the method itself; they're only exercised transitively through integration scenarios. `Engine::call_async` (R1 DX major #5 opt-out for per-tx IVM barrier), `Engine::read_view_with`, `Engine::handler_to_mermaid` (E7 deliverable, exit-criterion #5 touches it through DSL wrapper only), `Engine::handler_predecessors`, `Engine::metrics_snapshot`, `Engine::count_nodes_with_label`, `Engine::change_event_count`, `Engine::ivm_subscriber_count`, `Engine::read_current_version`, `Engine::test_subscribe_all_change_events`, `Engine::test_subscribe_change_events_matching_label`, `Engine::testing_insert_privileged_fixture`. Some are test-helpers (acceptable to be covered-by-use) but `call_async`, `read_view_with`, `handler_to_mermaid`, `handler_predecessors`, and the metric/counter accessors are public API that should have at least one direct test.
findings.evidence: Grep across `crates/*/tests/**` and `bindings/napi`, `packages/engine` shows no test named for these methods; usage exists in compromises_regression.rs (metrics_snapshot), exit_criteria (handler_to_mermaid via DSL), but no direct `call_async`-named test asserts its async opt-out contract against the per-tx IVM barrier race R1 DX #5 called out. `call_async` was the R1 DX-critic's fix for the IVM barrier race — its regression test is load-bearing.
findings.fix: Add `crates/benten-engine/tests/call_async_opt_out.rs` with at least: (1) `call_async_does_not_block_on_ivm_barrier` — spawn a slow IVM subscriber, prove `call_async` returns before IVM catches up; (2) `call_async_and_call_eventually_converge` — same final view state. Similarly add `handler_to_mermaid_rust_side_smoke.rs` so the Rust-level API is not only exercised via the TS `@benten/engine` wrapper. Minor-promote the test-helper accessors (`testing_insert_privileged_fixture`, `test_subscribe_*`) into internal-only `#[cfg(any(test, feature="test-support"))]` rather than leaving them on the public API.

findings.location: crates/benten-graph/src/lib.rs — RedbBackend label/property index + edge CRUD
findings.claim: `RedbBackend::get_by_label` and `RedbBackend::get_by_property` (plan G5, performance target <50µs hot-cache) have no dedicated test; `label_prop_index.rs` only exercises write-time index maintenance, not the public read-side accessor surface. `RedbBackend::put_edge` / `get_edge` / `edges_from` / `edges_to` are exercised only in `node_edge_store_blanket.rs` which is against `RedbBackend` inherent methods — the `NodeStore`/`EdgeStore` trait blanket impls over an arbitrary `KVBackend` (the point of G4, per R1 P1.graph.node-store-trait) are never tested generically. If a future peer-fetch `KVBackend` impl arrives, no test would fail on the trait contract.
findings.evidence: `crates/benten-graph/tests/label_prop_index.rs` tests 4 things: one populate+write, no explicit `get_by_label` / `get_by_property` read call or performance gate. `node_edge_store_blanket.rs` opens `RedbBackend::open(...)` directly — there is no `<B: KVBackend + NodeStore>`-generic test. Inspection of `crates/benten-graph/src/lib.rs:622–640` shows `NodeStore` + `EdgeStore` are defined as `pub trait`s but no test is generic over them.
findings.fix: Add `crates/benten-graph/tests/get_by_label_and_property_read_path.rs` with positive-case, empty-result, and (criterion-adjacent) hot-cache access-time tests. Add a `trait_conformance_generic` test using a simple `MemBackend` shim (already hinted at in `backend_error_polymorphism.rs`) to exercise `NodeStore`/`EdgeStore` as traits. This prevents silent trait-atrophy when a second `KVBackend` impl lands in Phase 2.

findings.claim: Per-view minimum per plan §4.1 is three categories: (a) build-from-scratch matches incremental, (b) stale-on-budget-exceeded, (c) write-read latency bound. Views 1, 2, and 5 each have only 4 shallow tests — shape-discriminant checks, id assertions, and single-update smoke tests. No equivalence proptest, no stale-on-budget test, no latency assertion. View 4 has 4 substantial tests (depth truncation, cycle no-infinite-loop, empty graph). Only View 3 approximates the plan — 5 tests plus `prop_content_listing_incremental_equivalence`.
findings.evidence: `grep -c '#\[test\]' crates/benten-ivm/tests/view{1,2,5}*.rs` returns 4 each. Inspection shows only ViewId / default / update-ok / rebuild-ok shape checks. `stale_on_budget_exceeded.rs` covers the stale-budget shape but asserts against `each_view_stale_on_budget_exceeded` which — reading the R3 stub — tests one representative view, not all five. No file asserts View1 / View2 / View5 hitting stale state.
findings.fix: Per plan §4.1, add for each of View1/View2/View5 these missing tests: `viewN_build_from_scratch_matches_incremental` (equivalence), `viewN_stale_on_budget_exceeded_marks_stale_and_rebuildable`, `viewN_write_read_latency_under_target`. View 4 should also add `view4_build_from_scratch_matches_incremental`. This is ~12 tests; critical for the IVM-is-the-load-bearing-subsystem thesis.

findings.location: crates/benten-core/src/lib.rs — free-function version-chain API vs method-form
findings.claim: The plan's C6 deliverable reserves four version-chain names: `walk_versions`, `current_version`, `append_version`, plus the `Anchor` constructor. On-disk in `crates/benten-core/src/lib.rs:642–666` there are two parallel shapes: free functions `pub fn append_version(_anchor, _version)`, `pub fn current_version(_anchor)`, `pub fn walk_versions(_anchor)` — each `todo!()`-bodied — AND a `versioning` module (line 682+) with `pub fn append_version(...)`, `pub fn walk_versions(_anchor) -> alloc::vec::IntoIter<Cid>`. Tests only go through the `versioning::` module form (`anchor_version.rs`, `version_branched.rs`); the top-level free functions have zero tests. This is a public-API-surface finding: either the free functions are meant to shadow (and deserve tests + removal of the module form) or they're dead API (and should be made private).
findings.evidence: `rg '^pub fn (append_version|current_version|walk_versions)' crates/benten-core/src/lib.rs` shows both the `pub fn` at crate root and another in `pub mod versioning`. `crates/benten-core/tests/anchor_version.rs` and `version_branched.rs` only call the module-form (`versioning::append_version`). No test calls `benten_core::append_version` directly.
findings.fix: R4b / R5 G1-B decision: either (1) flag the crate-root free functions as internal and hide them behind `#[doc(hidden)]` or remove them, or (2) add at least one test per direct-form function and document which is canonical. Tests that discriminate this explicitly prevent the module/free-fn drift.

findings.location: workspace — category vs R2 landscape projection
findings.claim: R2 projected: 201 unit / 14 proptest / 18 integration / 14 criterion / 11 Vitest / 22 security / 9 CI. Measured on disk: unit count is fine (the ~410 `#[test]` hits span all 94 test files and include 40 TRANSFORM rejection subtests; under-count unlikely). Proptest is 7/14 (-50%). CI workflows: 8/9 (-11%, tolerance). Vitest test count inside `packages/engine/src/*.test.ts` + `bindings/napi/index.test.ts` + `tools/create-benten-app/test/scaffolder.test.ts` is ~14 `it(...)` calls (close to the 11-Vitest plan). Security: R3 security writer shipped 14 files with 74 test functions (ahead of the 22-class plan). Integration: `tests/integration/*.rs` has 12 files and the `integration.rs` aggregator — matches the 13-qa-expert projection; ~45 test functions. Criterion: 10+4 bench functions landed — close to 14.
findings.evidence: Category-by-category tally: the proptest under-projection is the only >20% gap. Integration, security, unit, criterion, Vitest all within ±20%. Concern: the landscape's 'benten-ivm per-view: a+b+c pattern' (15 tests across 5 views = 15 unit) is under-delivered — see other finding.
findings.fix: Address the proptest gap (see finding #1) and the per-view-depth gap (see finding #5); rest of category coverage is healthy.

findings.claim: Invariants 5 and 6 each need the full quartet. Invariant 5 (nodes) has accepts_at_limit + rejects_over + single-node-passes + diagnostic-accessor (✓). Invariant 6 (edges) has rejects_over + diagnostic-accessor but no dedicated `accepts_edges_at_limit` test. The `single_node_subgraph_passes_node_and_edge_caps` test only exercises 0 edges, not the edge cap boundary. A regression where the edge cap is off-by-one (e.g., misreads 8192 as 8191) would not fire on any current test.
findings.evidence: Inspection of `invariants_5_6_counts.rs`: `accepts_nodes_at_limit` uses `DEFAULT_MAX_NODES`; no symmetric `accepts_edges_at_limit` with `DEFAULT_MAX_EDGES`. The `force_add_cross_edges_for_testing(edge_cap + 1)` exercises over-cap but no test uses `edge_cap` exactly.
findings.fix: Add `fn accepts_edges_at_limit()` that builds a subgraph with exactly `DEFAULT_MAX_EDGES` edges and asserts `build_validated()` returns Ok. One-test addition; no infrastructure needed.

findings.location: crates/benten-eval/tests/invariants_9_10_12.rs — missing positive-at-limit test for determinism invariant
findings.claim: Invariant 9 (determinism) has rejects_nondet_in_det_context (rejection) + accepts_det_primitives_in_det_context (positive), but no error-diagnostic-accessor check — the test only asserts `err.code()`, no context fields (`op_type`, `node_id`) are asserted. Comparison with invariants 1/2/3/5/6 which each include context-field accessors (`cycle_path`, `depth_actual`, `fanout_max`, `nodes_actual`, `edges_actual`).
findings.evidence: `invariants_9_10_12.rs::rejects_nondet_in_det_context` asserts only `err.code() == ErrorCode::InvDeterminism`. No `op_type` or `node_id` accessor call. `rejects_content_hash_mismatch` similarly lacks `expected_cid` / `actual_cid` accessor assertions even though `RegistrationError` exposes `expected_cid()` / `actual_cid()` methods at `crates/benten-eval/src/lib.rs:193–203`.
findings.fix: Extend the two existing `rejects_*` tests with the missing accessor assertions. Two one-line additions per test. Keeps the pattern consistent across the invariant-test family.

findings.claim: `LABEL_CURRENT` and `LABEL_NEXT_VERSION` public constants are load-bearing (version-chain walkers depend on exact spelling). No test asserts the literal string values. A rename would propagate silently if callers use the constant but no test locks the constant's string identity.
findings.evidence: `grep -n 'LABEL_CURRENT\|LABEL_NEXT_VERSION' crates/benten-core/tests/ bindings/napi/ packages/` returns zero hits in tests.
findings.fix: Add `tests/version_label_constants.rs` with two asserts: `assert_eq!(LABEL_CURRENT, "CURRENT")` and `assert_eq!(LABEL_NEXT_VERSION, "NEXT_VERSION")`. Protects against accidental rename.

findings.claim: `bindings/napi/tests/input_validation.rs` exists with 5 Rust-side tests per R3 security writer, but the napi source (`bindings/napi/src/lib.rs`) under the `napi-export` feature doesn't currently compile tests against `#[napi]` attributes (they're wrapped in the feature-flagged module `napi_surface`). If the feature isn't active in `cargo test`, the test-surface helpers (`deserialize_value_from_js_like`, `make_giant_map`, etc.) fall through `super::`-path functions that are public but stub. The tests will compile against the stubs and pass vacuously. Unclear whether R5 will toggle the feature in CI for the input-validation suite.
findings.evidence: Inspection of `bindings/napi/src/lib.rs:30` shows `#[cfg(feature = "napi-export")] mod napi_surface`. The public test-helper fns `deserialize_value_from_js_like`, `make_giant_map` (lines 239–275) are top-level, not under the feature gate — which is correct for test access, but the tests in `tests/input_validation.rs` were not read by this review to confirm assertion strength.
findings.fix: R4 reviewer `rust-test-reviewer` should inspect `bindings/napi/tests/input_validation.rs` for real E_INPUT_LIMIT-firing assertions vs. stub pass-through. If the assertions are vacuous, R3-red-phase is correct; R5 implementer must make them real. Flag for cross-reference with `rust-test-reviewer`'s R4 output.

findings.location: docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md — codes present in ERROR-CATALOG that map to Phase-1 but aren't explicitly catalog-referenced in any test string comparison
findings.claim: `E_CAP_ATTENUATION` (Phase-1-reachable via declared-vs-actual `requires` check on chained CALL) is referenced only via `error_code_mapping.rs::cap_error_codes_match_catalog` — no test calls the attenuation path and asserts this code fires. The R3 coverage stub lists it under covers_error_code with only that generic roundtrip. Similarly, `E_TRANSFORM_SYNTAX` is fired 16 times by `transform_grammar_rejections.rs` but the R3 stub's map shows the same 16 names — that's fine, but no test asserts the error carries `grammar_doc` context field beyond `transform_grammar_error_carries_doc_pointer` which is one test for all 38 rejection classes.
findings.evidence: `grep 'attenuation' crates/` returns `crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs` (internal mention) and `crates/benten-eval/tests/requires_enforcement.rs::handler_cannot_escalate_via_call_attenuation` — but that test asserts route `ON_DENIED` with `E_CAP_DENIED`, not `E_CAP_ATTENUATION`. `E_CAP_ATTENUATION` is not fired by any test assertion on disk.
findings.fix: Add one test `requires_property_call_attenuation_fires_e_cap_attenuation_when_child_scope_wider_than_parent` to `crates/benten-eval/tests/requires_enforcement.rs`. Ensures the declared-vs-actual attenuation semantics are locked.

findings.claim: R2 landscape projects 9 cross-cutting CI jobs (T4/T5/T6/T7/T8/T9 + supply-chain, cold-install, cross-process-graph). On disk: 8 workflow files. No dedicated `msrv.yml`; MSRV (Rust 1.85) testing is presumably a job inside `ci.yml` or `determinism.yml`. Without reading the YAML, the gate that `cargo build --locked` at MSRV reproduces the canonical CID fixture cannot be confirmed as a separate scheduled run.
findings.evidence: `ls .github/workflows/` → 8 files: ci.yml, cold-install.yml, cross-process-graph.yml, determinism.yml, drift-detect.yml, supply-chain-seeded-test.yml, supply-chain.yml, wasm-checks.yml. No msrv.yml.
findings.fix: Confirm with R3 performance-writer artifact `.addl/phase-1/r3-coverage-stub.json` line 601 (which lists `.github/workflows/determinism.yml#msrv (T5)` as a job-within-workflow). If so, this is fine; flag for confirmation not escalation. If MSRV isn't a job anywhere, escalate.

findings.claim: `ErrorCode::from_str` returns `ErrorCode::Unknown(String)` on catch-all per R1 drift-detector finding. `error_codes.rs` has 8 tests but it's unclear whether `from_str_accepts_unknown_variant_via_fallback` is one of them. Without that specific test the drift-detector's `ErrorCode::Unknown` fallback could regress silently.
findings.evidence: Test filenames visible only — could not read `crates/benten-core/tests/error_codes.rs` body in this review.
findings.fix: Verify presence; if absent, add `from_str_accepts_unknown_variant_via_fallback` and `drift_detector_unknown_variant_surfaces_as_fail_not_panic`. Load-bearing for R1 security-major-#11 drift-detector semantics.

coverage_confirmations.exit_criteria_mapped: all 6 have Rust integration + TS Vitest per R3 qa-expert manifest (tests/integration/exit_criteria_all_six.rs + packages/engine/src/crud.test.ts + trace.test.ts + mermaid.test.ts + bindings/napi/index.test.ts)

escalations: Confirm MSRV test coverage (determinism.yml#msrv job vs separate workflow) with R3 performance-writer manifest. r3-coverage-stub.json names it as a job-within-workflow; no standalone msrv.yml exists. Low risk but flag for R4b sanity check.

Namespace-overlap between `crates/benten-core/src/lib.rs` free-function `append_version` / `walk_versions` / `current_version` at crate root vs the same names under `pub mod versioning`. Tests only use the module form. Escalate to G1-B / R5 implementer for a choose-one-or-the-other decision; leave the other form under `#[doc(hidden)]` or remove.
